# Pecron Battery Monitor

> Open-source Python tool to monitor and control Pecron portable power stations from a Raspberry Pi, Linux server, or Mac — without the phone app.

## What this project does

This is a standalone Python application that monitors and controls Pecron portable power stations (battery generators). It connects directly to devices over local WiFi (TCP port 6607, AES-CBC encrypted) with automatic cloud MQTT fallback. Works offline for vanlife/off-grid use. Replaces the need for the Pecron phone app.

## Key capabilities

- Real-time battery monitoring (percentage, voltage, temperature, power in/out)
- Remote control of AC output, DC output, UPS mode, screen brightness
- Low-battery alerts via Telegram, ntfy, or webhooks
- Automation rules (turn off AC when battery below 10%, schedule shutoffs, etc.)
- Home Assistant MQTT integration with auto-discovery
- Runs 24/7 on a Raspberry Pi as a systemd service
- Supports all Pecron models that use the Pecron app (28+ models)
- Auto-detects device model and available controls via TSL (Thing Specification Language)
- **Local LAN monitoring** — talks directly to device over WiFi TCP (port 6607), no internet needed
- Cloud MQTT fallback when local connection is unavailable
- No phone or Bluetooth required

## Supported devices

Any Pecron power station compatible with the Pecron mobile app, including:
E300LFP, C300LFP Mini, E500LFP, E600LFP, E800LFP, E1000LFP, E1500LFP, E2000LFP, E2200LFP, E2400LFP, E3600, E3600LFP, E3800LFP, F1000LFP, F3000LFP, F5000LFP, WB12200

## Requirements

- Python 3.9+
- Pecron account (email/password)
- Device key (MAC address from Pecron app settings)

## Installation

```
git clone https://github.com/attractify-logan/pecron-monitor.git
cd pecron-monitor
pip install -r requirements.txt
python pecron_monitor.py --setup
```

## Documentation

Full documentation is in README.md including step-by-step installation, usage examples, alert setup, automation rules, Home Assistant integration, Raspberry Pi service setup, troubleshooting, and FAQ.
